fine-art-photoreal: Fine-art realistic pet portrait made for canvas. Believable pet anatomy, natural fur detail, accurate eyes, shallow depth of field, soft studio or window light, clean painterly photographic finish. Must look like a premium realistic canvas portrait, not glossy plastic AI art.
studio-canvas-realism: Premium studio-realistic canvas portrait. Pet is photographed/painted realistically against a simple tonal backdrop with professional lighting, sharp eyes, tactile fur detail, and subtle canvas-print sensibility. Avoid over-smoothed fur, perfect symmetry, and generic AI glamour.
cinematic-character-realism: Realistic cinematic pet portrait with character styling. Believable fur, natural eyes, costume or prop details integrated like a premium film still, dramatic but tasteful lighting, shallow depth and canvas-ready finish. Use archetypes only; no recognisable film/TV/game/comic IP.
natural-light-canvas: Natural-light realistic pet portrait for canvas. Soft outdoor or window light, believable coat texture, relaxed pet expression, clean background, gentle depth of field, premium editorial realism.
watercolour-floral: Soft watercolour painting with washy edges. Pet rendered with gentle pigment fades, warm cream paper background. Wildflowers (lavender, daisies, cornflowers, soft yellows) wrap loosely around the lower half of the composition. Pet's eyes carry the most detail.
oil-on-linen: Classical oil painting style on visible linen weave. Low-key chiaroscuro — dramatic single-source light from one side, deep shadows on the opposite. Warm muted background (burnt sienna, deep umber). Pet rendered with thick brushstroke texture, especially in fur and around eyes.
soft-pastel: Pastel chalk on textured paper. Soft, tender rendering — feathered edges, no hard lines. Warm cream-pink background. Pet's grey muzzle and eyes get the most detail; the rest fades into the cream.
renaissance-oil: Aged oil painting in the manner of 17th-century Dutch animal portraiture. Pet rendered with dignity and weight — head and chest filling the frame, eyes catching a single highlight. Background: deep velvet green or claret, slight visible canvas weave.
pop-art: Andy Warhol-style four-panel grid OR single-panel high-contrast pop. Bright saturated palette — magenta, cyan, yellow, orange. Bold black outlines around pet shape. Background flat colour, no gradient.
royal-oil: Pet dressed in mock-historical royal regalia (crown, cape, ruff) painted in the style of a court portrait. Heavy oil texture, gold-trimmed accents. Deep maroon or navy background. Pet's expression dignified — never silly.
cowboy-poster: Old west wanted-poster aesthetic. Sepia-toned, distressed paper texture. Pet centred, eyes intense. Bold serif headline at top ('WANTED' or pet name in caps). Subtitle below in smaller western type.
folk-linocut: Hand-carved folk linocut print. Bold black carved linework, visible ink grain, limited palette of cream, navy, rust, ochre, and warm tan. Decorative folk motifs can frame the pet, but the pet remains the subject.
mid-century-gouache: Mid-century gouache illustration with flat confident shapes, matte paint texture, warm retro palette, simplified but expressive pet anatomy, and strong poster-like composition.
neon-sign-portrait: Stylised neon sign portrait. Pet silhouette and key facial features built from glowing tube-like lines, saturated electric colour, dark simple backdrop, subtle bloom, graphic nightlife energy. Must read as illustration/sign art, not a photo.
risograph-summer-print: Bright risograph summer print. Limited ink layers, offset registration, grainy print texture, coral, butter yellow, sea blue, grass green, and cream. Flat graphic shapes with hand-drawn linework.
claymation-toy: Handmade claymation toy portrait. Soft sculpted clay forms, tiny fingerprints, matte stop-motion texture, rounded charming proportions, playful but premium. Must look like a crafted art object, not a plastic 3D render.
felted-wool-plush: Needle-felted wool plush portrait. Visible wool fibres, handmade textile texture, rounded soft sculptural forms, warm craft-paper or simple colour background, tactile and cosy. Must not look photographic.
